questions.question: What percentage of your total daily energy does your brain use?
questions.explanation: Your brain uses about 20% of your total daily energy, even though it makes up only about 2% of your body weight. This is why consistent fueling through regular meal timing is so critical for mental clarity.

questions.question: Why is eating within 1-2 hours of waking recommended for mental health?

options.text: It helps you burn more calories throughout the day
It signals to your brain that it is safe and resources are available, helping reduce morning cortisol spikes
It is required for proper digestion of lunch

questions.explanation: Eating within the first couple hours of waking signals to your brain that it is safe and resources are available. This helps reduce morning cortisol (stress hormone) spikes, setting a calmer tone for the day.

questions.question: How often should you ideally space your meals and snacks to prevent blood sugar crashes?
questions.explanation: Spacing meals and snacks every 3-4 hours prevents the blood sugar crashes that trigger anxiety, irritability, and cravings for quick-fix sugary foods.

questions.question: Why should you finish your last substantial meal 2-3 hours before bedtime?

options.text: To support better sleep quality, which is essential for mental health recovery and mood regulation

questions.explanation: Finishing your last substantial meal 2-3 hours before bedtime supports better sleep quality, which is essential for mental health recovery and mood regulation.

questions.question: What should you include at each meal to prevent the spike-and-crash energy cycle?
questions.explanation: Including protein, healthy fat, and fiber at each meal slows digestion and provides a steady stream of energy, preventing the spike-and-crash cycle that triggers anxiety and fatigue.

title: Meal Timing Template for Mental Energy - Quiz
